denial_pattern_sources: ignored_physician_letter: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey
peer_to_peer_window_verbal_only: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey; documented in multiple state PA reform laws (TX HB 2002, VA SB 1272)
denial_letter_text:
Dear Plan Member,

We received your provider's request on 04/20/2026 for CT pulmonary angiography related to Pulmonary embolism, suspected low-intermediate risk (I26.99). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Continuation of CT pulmonary angiography requires an active prior authorization; the prior auth lapsed. A new authorization must be submitted.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with continuation of care lapsed. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the verbal timeframe communicated to office staff (not confirmed in this letter).

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management

Standard criteria apply regardless of information previously sent to the plan.
clinical_context: The 69-year-old member has Pulmonary embolism, suspected low-intermediate risk (I26.99). She has been treated under commercial coverage with CT pulmonary angiography recommended as the next appropriate step. Wells score 4.5; hypoxia 91% on room air; D-dimer elevated. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that continuation of care lapsed bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
